# Semantic map

| Area | Normative source | Key contract |
|---|---|---|
| Reader, printer, canonical hash | [CoreForm canon/hash](../docs/spec/COREFORM_CANON_HASH.md) | Equivalent forms print and hash identically |
| Evaluation and module scope | [Module scope](../docs/spec/MODULE_SCOPE.md) | Pure lexical evaluation; top-level recursive `def` |
| Runtime values and effect hashes | [Value/effect hash](../docs/spec/VALUE_EFFECT_HASH.md) | Total, stable hashes for replay |
| Seals and dispatch | [Seals/dispatch/replay](../docs/spec/SEALS_DISPATCH_REPLAY.md) | Protocol values cannot be forged |
| Types | [Types](../docs/spec/TYPES.md) | Typechecking remains outside TCB-A |
| Standard library | [Foundation stdlib](../docs/FOUNDATION_STDLIB_v0.2.md) | Behavior in libraries, not kernel forms |
| Effects and concurrency | [Concurrency](../docs/spec/CONCURRENCY_v0.1.md) | Deterministic scheduling facts and explicit host work |
| Optimization | [Optimizer](../docs/spec/OPTIMIZER.md) | Semantics preserved by validation |
| Translation tiers | [Translation validation](../docs/spec/TRANSLATION_VALIDATION.md) | Same canonical behavior across tiers |

# GenesisCode v0.2 — Codex Working Agreement

## Mission
Implement GenesisCode v0.2 per `docs/PAPER_v0.2.md` and `docs/TECH_HANDOFF.md`.

## Non-negotiable invariants
- Kernel (Gλ) must be pure and deterministic. No filesystem/time/network/LLM inside the evaluator.
- UNHANDLED/EFFECT/ERROR must be unforgeable using seal tokens created by Prelude.
- Never panic on user input. Convert errors to sealed ERROR values at boundaries, or explicit Rust `Result` internally.
- Effect runner must be deny-by-default and must produce deterministic effect logs + replay checker.

## Repo conventions
- Language: Rust workspace in `crates/`.
- Keep TCB-A minimal: evaluator + immutable primitives + seals only.
- Prefer adding tests/goldens for every semantic rule.

## Development flow
- Make small commits (if/when you use git) with passing tests.
- Prefer implementing behavior in libraries over adding kernel special forms.

# `genesis` command surface

## Rules for automation

- Use `--json`; expect exactly one JSON object on stdout.
- Treat exit codes as stable categories, not generic success/failure.
- Use `--engine selfhost` on routed frontend commands.
- Pin the selfhost artifact identity in production paths.
- Retrieve recursive command/option definitions from `genesis cli-schema`.
- Never scrape human help when a JSON contract exists.

## Machine discovery

```bash
./target/debug/genesis cli-schema --json
./target/debug/genesis agent-index --help
./target/debug/genesis agent-plan --help
```

## Command families

{{< include generated/cli-commands.mdinc >}}

## Normative details

- [CLI flags, commands, exits, and JSON](../docs/spec/CLI.md)
- [CLI JSON schema IDs](../docs/spec/CLI_JSON_SCHEMAS_v0.1.md)
- [GCPM JSON schema IDs](../docs/spec/GCPM_JSON_SCHEMAS_v0.1.md)
- [GCPM workflow reports](../docs/spec/GCPM_WORKFLOW_REPORTS_v0.1.md)
- [WASI command parity](../docs/spec/WASI.md)

Usage: genesis [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  parse                Parse and canonicalize a CoreForm module without modifying its source file
  fmt                  Canonical formatting for CoreForm (.gc) files
  eval                 Evaluate a CoreForm program/module (pure)
  explain              Explain contract dispatch path for a given message
  run                  Run an effect program with a capability policy and emit a deterministic log
  replay               Replay an effect program deterministically from a log (hard-fails on mismatch)
  debug                Deterministic debug/trace commands for contract dispatch root-cause loops
  test                 Run package obligations (unit tests, determinism, replay checks, etc.) and write evidence into .genesis/store
  pack                 Compute and store a content-addressed package artifact record
  selfhost-artifact    Build a selfhost CoreForm toolchain artifact for bootstrap cutover
  selfhost-dashboard   Emit a selfhost cutover dashboard artifact and markdown mirror
  warm                 Warm startup mode: process versioned newline-delimited JSON frames in one process
  mcp                  Serve the pinned Model Context Protocol interface over stdio
  cli-schema           Emit machine-readable CLI command/option schema for agent planning
  agent-index          Emit AI-facing planning index or one exact symbol/diagnostic record
  agent-plan           Plan a deterministic workflow DAG from structured agent intent with policy prechecks
  bench                Predeclare, execute, validate, replay, bundle, and publish GenesisBench runs
  keygen               Generate a new Ed25519 signing key
  sign                 Sign a package acceptance artifact and record the signature in the evidence store
  transparency-verify  Verify the local transparency log chain (if present)
  typecheck            Run the type/effect checker for a package
  optimize             Optimize a CoreForm module/program (pure subset only)
  apply-patch          Validate and apply a semantic patch, then re-run package obligations
  session              Isolated, content-addressed agent transaction sessions
  semantic-edit        Semantic edit tooling for agentic patch planning [aliases: semedit]
  verify               Verify package hashes and evidence store integrity
  store                Content-addressed store operations (effectful; policy-gated)
  refs                 Manage refs (branches/tags) as effectful operations (policy-gated)
  commit               Create and inspect GenesisGraph commit artifacts
  pkg                  GenesisPkg/GCPM operations (snapshot + bundle export/import) [aliases: gcpm]
  policy               Local policy alias/default management
  sync                 Sync artifacts and refs with a remote registry (effectful; policy-gated)
  registry             First-party Genesis registry server operations
  gc                   Garbage-collect the local artifact store using reachability closure from refs + locks + pins
  vcs                  GenesisGraph commit DAG operations
  help                 Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --json
          Emit machine-readable JSON on stdout

      --step-limit <N>
          Kernel evaluation step limit (default is toolchain-defined)

      --no-step-limit
          Disable the kernel evaluation step limit

      --max-alloc-units <N>
          Maximum cumulative logical allocation units during evaluation

      --max-live-units <N>
          Maximum logical units reachable from evaluator roots at a safe point

      --max-pair-cells <N>
          Maximum total number of `pair/cons` cells allocated during evaluation

      --max-vec-len <N>
          Maximum observed vector length (vector literals and `vec/push`)

      --max-map-len <N>
          Maximum observed map length (map literals, `map/put`, `map/merge`)

      --max-bytes-len <N>
          Maximum observed bytes length (bytes literals and `bytes/concat`)

      --max-string-len <N>
          Maximum observed string length in UTF-8 bytes (string literals and `str/concat`)

      --selfhost-artifact <FILE>
          Path to selfhost toolchain artifact used when `--engine selfhost` is selected. Defaults to `./.genesis/selfhost/toolchain.gc` when bootstrap mode allows artifacts

      --selfhost-bootstrap <SELFHOST_BOOTSTRAP>
          Selfhost bootstrap mode. Accepted value: artifact-only.
          
          [default: artifact-only]
          [possible values: artifact-only]

      --selfhost-only
          Enforce selfhost-only execution for frontend paths.
          
          In this mode, commands that accept `--engine` must use `--engine selfhost`, and selfhost bootstrap mode must be `artifact-only` (no embedded fallback). This can also be enabled via `GENESIS_SELFHOST_ONLY=1`.

      --coreform-frontend <COREFORM_FRONTEND>
          CoreForm frontend. Accepted value: selfhost.

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

  -V, --version
          Print version

## `.gclog` {#gclog-c9c2fbf4}

<span class="status-chip">profile-selected</span>

**Signature:** `stable profile identifier: .gclog`

**Domains:** effects<br>
**Effects:** records deterministic runner effects<br>
**Capabilities:** artifact write capability during run only<br>
**Diagnostics:** EFFECT, ERROR, replay/mismatch, caps/denied

<details><summary>Contracts and examples</summary>

**Contracts**

- Effects are explicit Pure or Perform programs; kernel evaluation performs no host operation.
- The host runner denies every operation absent from caps.toml and records deterministic replay facts.
- Replay consumes entries in order and rejects operation, hash, decision, capability, or scheduler mismatch.

**Examples**

- **usage-template:** `stable profile identifier: .gclog` → `Identifier`

**Sources**

- `crates/gc_prelude/src/prelude.rs`: core/effect::pure, core/effect::perform, core/effect::bind
- `docs/spec/SEALS_DISPATCH_REPLAY.md`: Runner is deny-by-default, Every performed effect appends a deterministic log entry

</details>

**Capabilities:** none<br>
**Diagnostics:** genesis/error-v0.2, genesis/diagnostics-schema-v1

<details><summary>Contracts and examples</summary>

**Contracts**

- User input failures return explicit Rust Result internally, sealed ERROR values at runtime boundaries, or structured CLI diagnostics.
- UNHANDLED, EFFECT, and ERROR recognition requires trusted Prelude-created seals.
- CLI failures use genesis/error-v0.2 plus genesis/diagnostics-schema-v1 and stable nonzero exit classes.
- Every emitted CLI code resolves to one versioned genesis/diagnostic/v1 record with phase, span contract, structured parameters, causes, safe repairs, documentation, and source authorities; unknown codes fail closed.

Usage: genesis eval [OPTIONS] <FILE>

Arguments:
  <FILE>
          

Options:
      --engine <ENGINE>
          Frontend engine. Accepted value: selfhost.

      --json
          Emit machine-readable JSON on stdout

      --stage1-pipeline
          Run the Stage-1 compiler pipeline (CoreForm -> CoreForm validated transforms) before evaluation

      --step-limit <N>
          Kernel evaluation step limit (default is toolchain-defined)

      --no-step-limit
          Disable the kernel evaluation step limit

      --stage1-gate
          Require `core/obligation::stage1-validation` to pass for the Stage-1 pipeline. Implies `--stage1-pipeline`

      --max-alloc-units <N>
          Maximum cumulative logical allocation units during evaluation

      --stage2-gate
          Require stage-2 CoreForm->WASM translation validation to pass when this module is supported by stage-2 lowering

      --max-live-units <N>
          Maximum logical units reachable from evaluator roots at a safe point

      --max-pair-cells <N>
          Maximum total number of `pair/cons` cells allocated during evaluation

      --max-vec-len <N>
          Maximum observed vector length (vector literals and `vec/push`)

      --max-map-len <N>
          Maximum observed map length (map literals, `map/put`, `map/merge`)

      --max-bytes-len <N>
          Maximum observed bytes length (bytes literals and `bytes/concat`)

      --max-string-len <N>
          Maximum observed string length in UTF-8 bytes (string literals and `str/concat`)

      --selfhost-artifact <FILE>
          Path to selfhost toolchain artifact used when `--engine selfhost` is selected. Defaults to `./.genesis/selfhost/toolchain.gc` when bootstrap mode allows artifacts

      --selfhost-bootstrap <SELFHOST_BOOTSTRAP>
          Selfhost bootstrap mode. Accepted value: artifact-only.
          
          [default: artifact-only]
          [possible values: artifact-only]

      --selfhost-only
          Enforce selfhost-only execution for frontend paths.
          
          In this mode, commands that accept `--engine` must use `--engine selfhost`, and selfhost bootstrap mode must be `artifact-only` (no embedded fallback). This can also be enabled via `GENESIS_SELFHOST_ONLY=1`.

      --coreform-frontend <COREFORM_FRONTEND>
          CoreForm frontend. Accepted value: selfhost.

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')
